perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Each party shall make available confidential information to its employees only in so far as the employees need it to fulfil the purpose as set out in 1. above. It will ensure that these employ ees are equally obliged to observe secrecy, whereby the obligation shall survive the termination of their respective employment contracts.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Each party shall make available confidential information to its employees only in so far as the employees need it to fulfil the purpose as set out in 1. above. It will ensure that these employees are not obliged to observe secrecy, and the obligation shall not survive the termination of their respective employment contracts.
perturbation.explanation: By negating the obligation of employees to observe secrecy and removing the survival clause after termination, the changed text directly contradicts the intended protection of confidential information. This contradicts the fundamental purpose of the agreement outlined in section 1.1, which aims to keep information secret and confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Purpose 1.1 The parties intend to provide each other with certain information of the Technology Hubs in secret and confidential nature pertaining to their business proposal and/or its operations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 5.1 explicitly states that employees are 'not obliged to observe secrecy' and that the obligation does 'not survive the termination of their respective employment contracts.' This directly contradicts section 1.1, which states that the parties intend to provide information of a 'secret and confidential nature.' The change fundamentally undermines the confidential nature that section 1.1 aims to protect, creating a clear and direct conflict. The explanation provided states that this constitutes an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 The intellectual property rights (where applicable) relating to any confidential information disclosed in terms of this agreement shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and the disclosure shall not confer any license or right to such information on the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 The intellectual property rights (where applicable) relating to any confidential information disclosed in terms of this agreement shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and the disclosure shall automatically confer a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use such information on the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: This modification changes the effect of the disclosure of confidential information. The original text explicitly states that disclosure does not confer any license or right to the recipient. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that disclosure automatically grants a non-exclusive, royalty-free license. This directly impacts section 3.3, which restricts the recipient's use of confidential information, creating a conflict of obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 Beyond this, each party also undertakes as follows: 3.3.1 not to use the confidential information of the other party for any purposes other than the purpose contemplated by this agreement; 3.3.2 not to modify or reconstruct, cause others to reconstruct or use for any purposes other than the purpose contemplated by this agreement, any object made ava ilable to it by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6.1 now states that disclosure of confidential information automatically grants a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to the recipient. This directly contradicts section 3.3, which prohibits the recipient from using the confidential information for any purposes other than those contemplated by the agreement. The original text in 6.1 explicitly stated that disclosure does not confer any license, but the changed text directly opposes this, creating a clear inconsistency and thus an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1 No oral side agreements have been ma de. Any changes or additions to this Agreement must be made in writing to be effective. The written form requirement shall not be fulfilled by the use of emails.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1 Oral side agreements are permitted and binding. Any changes or additions to this Agreement can be made orally or in writing to be effective. The written form requirement shall be fulfilled by the use of emails.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by directly opposing the original clause regarding oral agreements and written modifications. The original text prohibits oral side agreements and requires changes to be in writing, explicitly excluding emails as sufficient written form. The altered text permits oral side agreements, allows oral or written modifications, and accepts emails as fulfilling the written form requirement. This creates complete uncertainty about how the agreement can be amended and enforced. Additionally, This contradicts section 3.2, which restricts the recipient's use of confidential information, creating a conflict of obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 Beyond this, each party also undertakes as follows: 3.3.1 not to use the confidential information of the other party for any purposes other than the purpose contemplated by this agreement; 3.3.2 not to modify or reconstruct, cause others to reconstruct or use for any purposes other than the purpose contemplated by this agreement, any object made ava ilable to it by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 10.1 directly contradicts the original text and, more importantly, creates a conflict with section 3.3. The original text prohibits oral agreements and requires written modifications, while the changed text permits oral agreements and allows oral or written modifications, including emails. This creates uncertainty about how the agreement can be amended. The original text also restricts the recipient's use of confidential information, while the changed text is silent on this point. Thus, there is a contradiction in terms of modification and enforcement of the confidentiality clause, a strong contradiction within the document.
